title: thymus serpyllum subsp. trachselianus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. trachselianus, also known as Wild Thyme, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Wild Thyme lie in its essential oils, which contain compounds like thymol and carvacrol, exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Wild Thyme include its ability to soothe digestive issues, boost the immune system, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating respiratory infections and skin conditions. Wild Thyme can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in cooking and traditional medicine to leverage its medicinal properties.
